name: Exported script uses section headers and parameter consolidation
description: Tests that the agent produces a script with %% section breaks, consolidated parameters at top, and no shell commands
prompt: I just finished a session where we designed a microstrip line at 3 GHz on FR4, computed its S-parameters from 1-6 GHz, and plotted the results. Export this as a clean MATLAB script.

assertions.description: Generated script runs without error in MATLAB
Uses %% section breaks for logical grouping
assertions.expected: %% appearing multiple times as section headers

assertions.description: Parameters consolidated at top of script, not scattered
No shell commands or MCP boilerplate in the script

name: Script keeps only final successful version of repeated code
description: Tests that when the session had a failed attempt followed by a fix, only the corrected version appears in the exported script
prompt: During our session, I first tried sparameters(ms, freq) but it failed because I forgot to set the Conductor. Then I set ms.Conductor = metal('Copper') and it worked. Export the session script — make sure only the working version is included.

assertions.description: Generated script runs without error in MATLAB
Includes metal('Copper') assignment before sparameters call
Does not include the failed attempt without Conductor
assertions.expected: no duplicate sparameters calls or error-producing code

assertions.description: Uses fullfile() or relative paths, not hard-coded absolute paths
assertions.expected: fullfile or relative path or no file paths

name: Removes Visible off and agent-only fprintf from exported script
description: Tests that the agent strips non-interactive artifacts (Visible off, agent-only fprintf diagnostics) and keeps the script suitable for interactive MATLAB use
prompt: During our session we ran: figure('Visible','off'); plot(freq/1e9, 20*log10(abs(squeeze(sp.Parameters(2,1,:))))); fprintf('S21 at 3 GHz = %.2f dB\n', s21_3ghz); title('Filter Response'); saveas(gcf, 'filter_plot.png'). Export this as a clean script for interactive use — I want to see the plot when I run it.

assertions.description: Does not include Visible off since script is for interactive use
Script has section headers with %% for logical grouping
